# QC Monitor — Backtest Report (Phases 1–6)

*Generated by `scripts/backtest/{reconstruct_history,detect_crashes,signal_timing,
payoff,calibrate,validate}.py`. Regenerate by re-running those scripts. Data lives
in `backtest/data/`.*

This tests whether the monitor's indicators would have flagged the major market
crashes of the last ~19 years at a useful time. **Reminder:** the tool is a
*buy-the-panic timer*, not a crash predictor — so the question is whether each
indicator went red **near the bottom**, whether buying then **paid off**, and
whether that holds up **out-of-sample**.

---

## Data foundation (Phase 1)

- **4,880 trading days reconstructed**, 2007-01-03 → today (back to KRE's 2006
  inception; captures the 2008 GFC).
- Levels recomputed with the **exact production thresholds** (imported from
  `market_monitor.py`).
- Validated: matches the live CSV on overlapping days; `^TNX` range 0.50–5.25%
  (no scaling bug; 5.25% = the 2007 peak); VIX 82.69 on 2020-03-16.

## Crash catalog (Phase 2) — 12 events

| # | Event | Type | Peak → Trough | Depth | Recovery |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | GFC 2008 | broad bear | 2007-10-09 → 2009-03-09 | **−56.8%** | 2013-03-28 |
| 2 | GFC 2008 (banks) | sector | 2009-05-08 → 2009-06-23 | −24.2% | 2010-01-08 |
| 3 | 2010 flash crash | sector | 2010-04-23 → 2010-06-08 | −20.5% | 2012-03-15 |
| 4 | 2011 EU/US debt | sector | 2011-04-28 → 2011-08-19 | −27.0% | 2012-02-03 |
| 5 | 2015 China/oil | broad | 2015-05-21 → 2016-02-11 | −14.2% | 2016-07-11 |
| 6 | 2018 Q4 (Feb) | broad | 2018-01-26 → 2018-02-08 | −10.2% | 2018-08-24 |
| 7 | 2018 Q4 selloff | broad | 2018-09-20 → 2018-12-24 | −19.8% | 2019-04-23 |
| 8 | COVID | broad bear | 2020-02-19 → 2020-03-23 | −33.9% | 2020-08-18 |
| 9 | 2022 bear | broad bear | 2022-01-03 → 2022-10-12 | −25.4% | 2024-01-19 |
| 10 | 2023 banks (SVB) | sector | 2023-02-07 → 2023-05-04 | −43.8% | 2024-11-06 |
| 11 | Aug-2024 VIX spike | vol spike | 2024-07-16 → 2024-08-05 | −8.5% | 2024-09-19 |
| 12 | 2025 tariff | broad | 2025-02-19 → 2025-04-08 | −18.9% | 2025-06-27 |

Broad = S&P ≥10% drawdown; sector = rolling 21-day return ≤ −15% the broad market
shrugged off; Aug-2024 curated (too brief for the rules).

## Signal timing (Phase 3)

Hit rate across the 12 crashes, and how noisy each indicator is the rest of the time:

| Indicator | Red | Amber+ | Red days in-crash | outside | % real |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| VIX | 11/12 | 12/12 | 333 | 116 | 74% |
| KRE | 8/12 | 10/12 | 94 | 3 | **97%** |
| QQQ | 7/12 | 12/12 | 388 | 72 | 84% |
| NVDA | 8/12 | 11/12 | 630 | 442 | **59%** |
| Brent (spike) | 1/12 | 3/12 | 35 | 0 | 100% (only 2008) |
| US10Y | 0/12 | 2/12 | 0 | 0 | never red |

In slow bears the drawdown signals fire *hundreds* of days early (VIX went red 483
days before the GFC bottom). Overall aggregate non-green **83% of days** (incl.
`brent_low`).

## Payoff (Phase 4) — quality basket forward return after the RED signal

Entry = first panic-red in each crash window. Average across the 12 crashes:

| basket | +1m | +3m | +6m | +12m |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Quality watchlist (any-red entry)** | −1.3% | +0.1% | +2.7% | **+10.1%** |
| Mag 7 (control) | +2.3% | +7.3% | +22.8% | +31.6% |
| Broad large-cap (control) | −0.0% | +3.8% | +8.9% | +15.3% |
| S&P 500 (benchmark) | −0.1% | +3.2% | +7.9% | +11.4% |
| *quality, bought at the exact bottom* | +11.9% | +18.2% | +23.4% | +37.4% |

As wired (act on any single red), the quality basket roughly **matches/trails the
index** — the stocks are fine (+37% bought at the bottom), the *entry timing* is the
problem. (Mag 7's number is inflated by survivorship + missing pre-IPO members.)

## Calibration (Phase 5)

Quality forward returns by trigger (`lead` = avg days before the bottom):

| trigger | fires on | +1m | +3m | +6m | +12m |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| any 1 red | 12/12 | −1.3% | +0.1% | +2.7% | +10.1% |
| ≥2 reds at once | 9/12 | +0.6% | +4.8% | +7.9% | +16.7% |
| **≥3 reds at once** | 7/12 | +5.9% | +8.5% | +12.6% | **+19.7%** |
| VIX ≥ 30 | 11/12 | −0.4% | +2.8% | +6.2% | +12.4% |
| VIX ≥ 35 | 10/12 | +0.9% | +4.8% | +5.8% | +18.4% |
| VIX ≥ 40 | 6/12 | −4.5% | +2.8% | +3.4% | +17.3% |
| *bought at the bottom* | 12/12 | +11.9% | +18.2% | +23.4% | +37.4% |

**Requiring broad confirmation is the fix** and it holds over 19 years: ≥3 reds
(+19.7%) and VIX ≥ 35 (+18.4%) clearly beat acting on a single red (+10.1%) and the
index (+11.4%). Noise: dropping `brent_low` cuts non-green days 83% → 54%;
edge-triggering yields **~6 alert episodes/year** instead of a near-daily ping.

## Extended validation (Phase 6)

**(1) Out-of-sample (the overfitting test).** Train on the 7 crashes ≤2018, test on
the 5 from 2020 on. The best rule chosen on training data only — **VIX ≥ 35
(+16.2% in-sample)** — then delivered **+21.6% out-of-sample** on crashes it never
saw (vs ~+11% index). Every confirmation rule beat "any 1 red" in *both* periods.
→ The core finding is **not an artifact of hindsight.**

**(2) Every firing of the ≥3-reds signal** (all history, not just catalogued
crashes): **27 firings, 22 positive at +12m, average +18.4%.** Honest catch: **all 5
losers were 2008** (early GFC firings, −4% to −41%) — in a prolonged systemic
collapse the signal fires repeatedly on the way down and stays underwater for a year.

**(3) Beat-luck.** Random buy day → +12.7% at 12m (markets drift up). The ≥3-reds
signal → +18.4%, beating **61%** of random days. A **real but modest** edge.

---

## Key findings

1. **Require broad confirmation, never a single red.** Across 19 years and 12
   crashes, ≥3 reds / VIX ≥ 35 beat both a single-red trigger and the index — and it
   **holds out-of-sample**. This is the single most important result.
2. **It's a modest edge, not a money printer.** +18.4% vs +12.7% random. Useful, not
   magic.
3. **No signal saves you in a 2008-style meltdown.** The signal fired early and
   often through 2008 and every one of those buys lost money for a year. **Stagger
   buys; the signal leads the bottom.**
4. **KRE is the cleanest signal** (97% of its red days were real crises); **NVDA the
   noisiest** (59%). Brent > $130 fired once (2008); US10Y > 5.5% never.
5. **Edge-triggering + dropping `brent_low`** turns a near-daily ping into ~6
   meaningful alerts/year.

## Recommended configuration *(implemented in `market_monitor.py`)*

1. **KAUFSIGNAL requires ≥3 panic indicators red at once** (broad selling) — VIX ≥ 35
   is a validated simpler alternative.
2. **Edge-trigger** alerts (notify on the transition, re-arm on reset).
3. **Drop `brent_low`** from the buy aggregate.
4. **Retire** the never-fired red bands cosmetically (Brent > $130 fired only in 2008;
   US10Y > 5.5% never) — left in place since they're harmless.
5. VIX / KRE / QQQ are the workhorses; NVDA confirming-only.

## Caveats

- **12 events is still a small sample**, dominated by a few big crises. Directional,
  not statistical proof.
- **The 2008 lesson is real:** in a long systemic crash the signal fires too early
  repeatedly. Stagger entries; don't go all-in on the first alert.
- **Mag 7 / control baskets** carry survivorship bias and (pre-2012/2010) missing
  members; treat as rough context, not strategies.
- **European holdings** are in local currency (EUR), not FX-converted.
- **Hurricane** indicator excluded (not backtestable from current sources).
